SEARCH
Package com.renomad.minum.web

Class WebFramework

java.lang.Object
com.renomad.minum.web.WebFramework
public final class WebFramework extends Object
Responsible for the logistics after socket connection

Relies on server sockets built in WebEngine. Builds a function in makePrimaryHttpHandler() that sort-of fits into a slot in WebEngine and handles HTTP protocol. Also handles routing and static files.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addMimeForSuffix(String suffix, String mimeType)
    This allows users to add extra mappings between file suffixes and mime types, in case a user needs one that was not provided.

    Similar to registerPath(StartLine.Verb, String, Function) except that the paths registered here may be partially matched.

  • Method Details

    • registerPath

      public void registerPath(StartLine.Verb verb, String pathName, Function<Request,Response> webHandler)
      Add a new handler in the web application for a combination of a StartLine.Verb, a path, and then provide the code to handle a request.
      Note that the path text expected is *after* the first forward slash, so for example with http://foo.com/mypath, you provide us "mypath" here.

    • registerPartialPath

      public void registerPartialPath(StartLine.Verb verb, String pathName, Function<Request,Response> webHandler)
      Similar to registerPath(StartLine.Verb, String, Function) except that the paths registered here may be partially matched.

      For example, if you register 

      .well-known/acme-challenge
      then it can match a client request for 
      .well-known/acme-challenge/HGr8U1IeTW4kY_Z6UIyaakzOkyQgPr_7ArlLgtZE8SX

      What if I didn't have the ability to match partial paths? In that case, if I tried 

      GET .well-known/acme-challenge/sadoifpiefewsfae
      I would get an StartLine.PathDetails.isolatedPath() of 
      .well-known/acme-challenge/sadoifpiefewsfae
      which wouldn't match anything I could statically register.

      Be careful here, be thoughtful - partial paths will

    • addMimeForSuffix

      public void addMimeForSuffix(String suffix, String mimeType)
      This allows users to add extra mappings between file suffixes and mime types, in case a user needs one that was not provided.

      This is made available through the web framework.

      Example: 

       webFramework.addMimeForSuffix().put("foo","text/foo")
